analysis.reasoning: Core message: EU leaders discuss COVID-19 response via VTC; stress international solidarity on vaccine access; coordination with WHO's Tedros on COVAX; the EU will contribute. Evaluation: EU1 (successful story) – not emphasized; no historical success or overcoming past mistakes. EU2 (EU is united) – implicit coordination among EU leaders, but unity/agreements are not explicitly highlighted; weak fit, not promoted. EU3 (useful for its citizens) – focus is global vaccine access, not specifically benefits within the EU; not applicable. EU4 (avant-garde actor) – no claim of pioneering regulation; not applicable. EU5 (global champion of fair causes) – touches solidarity but framed around pandemic logistics, not rights/democracy; not applicable. EU6 (EU contributes to a better world) – clear: promoting international solidarity, engaging with WHO/COVAX, and pledging the EU will play its part to overcome the pandemic globally; aligns with multilateralism and global well-being.
